name: US Great Plains Wheat Belt (Kansas)
capacity: ~7% of global wheat exports (hard red winter)
note: Dominant hard red winter wheat zone; risk: drought (La Nina), freeze-thaw heaving in spring

name: Canadian Prairies Wheat Belt (Saskatchewan)
note: Spring wheat and durum hub; risk: early frost, periodic drought on open prairie

name: Krasnodar / Rostov Wheat Belt
note: Largest single exporting nation; risk: spring drought, frost, geopolitical Black Sea disruption

name: Ukraine Winter Wheat Belt
note: High-yield black-soil steppe; risk: ongoing conflict, Odesa port blockade

name: Beauce Plain (France)
capacity: ~5% of global wheat exports; EU's largest wheat region
note: Soft wheat for milling and feed; risk: late spring drought, summer heat waves

name: Western Australia Wheatbelt
capacity: ~10% of global wheat exports in good years
note: Southern hemisphere counter-seasonal supply; risk: Indian Ocean Dipole drought, salinity

name: Argentine Pampas Wheat Belt
note: Southern hemisphere supplier; risk: La Nina drought, export taxes (retenciones)

name: India Punjab / Haryana Wheat Belt
note: Green Revolution heartland; risk: heat stress at grain-fill, groundwater depletion

name: US Corn Belt (Iowa / Illinois)
capacity: ~30% of global corn production; ~35% of exports
note: World's largest corn production complex; risk: summer drought, derecho storms

name: Mato Grosso Safrinha Corn Belt
region: Mato Grosso / Mato Grosso do Sul / Parana
capacity: ~14% of global corn exports; second crop after soy
note: Double-cropping safrinha system drives cost competitiveness; risk: delayed soy harvest compresses planting window

name: Argentina Pampas Corn Belt
note: Major supplier Apr-Jun; risk: La Nina reduces yields by 20-30% in severe years

name: Ukraine Corn Belt (Black Soil zone)
note: Deep chernozem soils; risk: conflict damage to storage, port access

name: Northeast China Corn Belt
note: China's corn basket feeds massive livestock sector; risk: spring flooding, early frost

name: Mato Grosso Soybean Belt
capacity: ~20% of global soybean production; Brazil total ~38%
note: Cerrado frontier expansion; risk: deforestation pressure, El Nino drought, logistics

name: US Soybean Belt (Iowa / Illinois)
capacity: ~28% of global soybean production; ~30% of exports
note: Rotation with corn; risk: summer drought, trade policy (China tariffs)

name: Argentine Pampas Soybean Belt (Rosario hub)
capacity: ~14% of global soy exports as meal+oil+beans
note: Largest soy processing cluster globally (Rosario crush corridor); risk: La Nina, export taxes

name: Paraguay Eastern Soybean Belt
note: Landlocked; exports via Parana river or Brazil ports; risk: river drought cuts barge transport

name: India Punjab / Haryana Rice Belt
note: Basmati rice premium export; risk: groundwater depletion, heat at flowering

name: India West Bengal / Eastern Plains Rice Belt
note: Multiple crops per year; risk: Bay of Bengal cyclones, flooding

name: Vietnam Mekong Delta Rice Belt
note: Three-crop system; risk: saltwater intrusion from Mekong low-flow years, upstream dams

name: Thailand Central Plains Rice Belt
note: Fragrant Hom Mali rice price premium; risk: El Nino drought reduces 2nd crop

name: China Hunan / Yangtze Basin Rice Belt
note: Double-rice heartland; risk: summer flooding (Yangtze), heavy metal soil contamination

name: Minas Gerais Coffee Belt (Brazil)
region: Minas Gerais / Sao Paulo / Espirito Santo
note: World's dominant producer; risk: biennial cycle, frost (July), drought

name: Vietnam Dak Lak Coffee Plateau
capacity: ~18% of global coffee production; #1 robusta exporter
note: Cheap robusta for instant coffee; risk: El Nino dry season stress

name: Colombia Coffee Belt (Eje Cafetero)
capacity: ~8% of global coffee production; mild washed arabica
note: Year-round harvest due to altitude; risk: La Nina excess rain causes leaf rust

name: Ethiopia Sidamo / Yirgacheffe Coffee Belt
capacity: ~5% of global coffee production; birthplace of arabica
note: Heirloom varieties command specialty premiums; risk: drought, political instability

name: Cote d'Ivoire Cocoa Belt
note: Single-country dominance; risk: swollen shoot virus, black pod disease, EUDR rules

name: Ghana Cocoa Belt (Ashanti / Brong-Ahafo)
capacity: ~13% of global cocoa production; premium quality
note: COCOBOD quality control supports premium; risk: Harmattan dry spells, aging tree stock

name: Ecuador Cocoa Belt (Los Rios / Guayas)
capacity: ~6% of global cocoa exports; #1 in fine-flavor
note: Unique fine-flavor Nacional variety; risk: El Nino flooding, frosty pod rot

name: Nigeria Cocoa Belt (Southwest)
note: 4th largest producer but declining investment; risk: poor post-harvest infrastructure

name: Brazil Sao Paulo Center-South Sugarcane Belt
note: Flex-fuel mills pivot between sugar and ethanol; risk: frost (rare), drought

name: India Uttar Pradesh Sugarcane Belt
capacity: ~17% of global sugar production; periodic export surplus
note: State-controlled cane price (SAP) distorts output; risk: monsoon failure

name: Thailand Northeast Sugarcane Belt
capacity: ~7% of global sugar exports (3rd largest exporter)
note: Rainy season crop; risk: El Nino drought sharply cuts cane yield and sucrose

name: Sumatra Palm Oil Belt
capacity: ~30% of global palm oil production (Sumatra alone)
note: Indonesia ~55% of world supply total; risk: peatland fire/haze, EUDR rules

name: Kalimantan Palm Oil Belt
note: Frontier expansion into carbon-rich peatlands; risk: EUDR compliance, fire season

name: Sabah / Sarawak Palm Oil Belt (Malaysia)
capacity: ~27% of global palm oil exports; Malaysia #2 producer
note: Premium GHG-certified supply emerging; risk: labor shortage, EUDR supply chain rules

name: Xinjiang Cotton Belt
capacity: ~22% of global cotton production; ~85% of China's output
note: Machine-harvested long-staple; risk: sanctions (UFLPA), water scarcity (Tarim)

name: India Deccan Cotton Belt
capacity: ~23% of global cotton production (largest by area)
note: Smallholder Bt cotton dominant; risk: pink bollworm resistance, erratic monsoon

name: Texas High Plains Cotton
note: Dryland and irrigated Upland cotton; risk: Ogallala aquifer depletion, drought, hail

name: Brazil Cerrado Cotton Belt (Mato Grosso)
capacity: ~7% of global cotton exports; fastest-growing exporter
note: Second crop after soy; high-tech mechanized; risk: logistics distance to ports

name: Thailand Southern Rubber Belt
note: World's largest producer; risk: drought, falling prices vs synthetic, aging trees

name: Indonesia Sumatra / Kalimantan Rubber Belt
note: Smallholder dominated; risk: competition with palm oil for land

name: Vietnam Southeast Rubber Plantations
region: Binh Phuoc / Dak Lak / Tay Ninh
note: State-led expansion; also rubber in Laos/Cambodia via VRG; risk: dry season stress

name: Saskatchewan Potash Basin
capacity: ~32% of global potash production; largest reserves
note: World's largest mineable potash reserve; risk: mine flooding, rail logistics

name: Soligorsk Potash Mines (Belarus)
note: Sanctions-hit since 2021; rerouted via Russian ports; risk: sanctions, mine subsidence

name: Berezniki / Solikamsk Potash (Ural)
note: Rich Verkhnekamsk deposit; risk: sinkhole events, sanctions reducing export routes

name: Dead Sea Potash Works (Israel)
capacity: ~7% of global potash via solar evaporation
note: Solar evaporation ponds unique; Dead Sea shrinking raises long-term brine risk

name: Khouribga Phosphate Belt (OCP Morocco)
capacity: ~14% of phosphate rock; ~35% of global reserves
note: Morocco controls ~70% of world's economic reserves; OCP is global price-setter

name: Florida Phosphate District
note: Largest non-Moroccan single district; risk: reclamation costs, phosphogypsum stacks

name: Yunnan / Guizhou Phosphate Basin
note: China restricts exports to protect domestic fertilizer supply; risk: ore grade decline

name: US Gulf Coast Ammonia / Nitrogen Hub
note: Henry Hub gas price sets cost floor; risk: hurricane disruption, gas price spikes

name: Trinidad Ammonia Complex (Point Lisas)
note: Atlantic Basin's key ammonia hub; risk: mature gas fields declining

name: Qatar Ammonia / Urea Complex (Mesaieed)
note: North Field gas underpins ultra-low feedstock cost; risk: Gulf tensions, Hormuz

name: Russia Ammonia Belt (Volga)
note: Togliatti-Odesa ammonia pipeline severed by war; risk: sanctions, gas price linkage

name: Port of Santos (agro export)
capacity: ~28% of global soybean exports via Santos+Paranagua
note: Latin America's largest port; also corn, sugar; risk: BR-163 bottleneck, draft limit

name: US Gulf Export Complex (New Orleans)
note: World's busiest grain export corridor; risk: Mississippi low water, hurricanes

name: Rosario / Up-River Port Complex
note: World's largest soy crush corridor; risk: Parana waterway drought, export tax policy

name: Novorossiysk Grain Terminal
note: Primary Russian wheat export terminal; risk: Black Sea naval incidents, storm queues

name: Odesa / Chornomorsk Port Complex
capacity: ~12% of global wheat + corn exports pre-war
note: Critical hub operating via grain-corridor workarounds; risk: missile strikes, naval mines

name: Port of Paranagua
note: Key complement to Santos; risk: Serra do Mar road disruption, harvest congestion

name: Russia Volga / Ural Wheat Belt
note: High interannual yield variability; risk: dry continental climate, late spring frost

name: Egypt Nile Delta Wheat Belt
note: High domestic demand far exceeds production; risk: Nile water (GERD), heat at grain fill

name: China North China Plain Wheat Belt
note: Winter wheat zone; state reserve replenishment sets price floor; risk: aquifer depletion

name: Jordan Phosphate Mines
note: Key supplier to India; exports via Aqaba; risk: water scarcity, regional geopolitics

name: Kola Peninsula Apatite (Russia)
capacity: ~7% of global phosphate rock; low-cadmium ore
note: EU favors Kola over Moroccan ore for low Cd; risk: sanctions limiting PhosAgro exports

name: Kandla / Mundra Port (India Agro Gateway)
capacity: ~4% of global wheat exports when India permits
note: Primary west-coast gateway for wheat/cotton exports; risk: India export ban policy

name: Pacific Northwest Wheat Export Hub
note: Gateway to Asian markets; risk: Columbia River barge low water
